benefits: Helps with Anti-Aging, Hydrating
ingredient_definition: A synthetic peptide that stimulates the production of six major structural proteins in the skin — collagen I, III, and IV, fibronectin, hyaluronic acid, and laminin-5 — making it one of the more comprehensively acting anti-aging peptides available. It helps visibly reduce the depth of wrinkles, improve firmness, and support the extracellular matrix for a more youthful skin architecture with regular use.

benefits: Helps with Dark Spots, Helps with Anti-Aging
ingredient_definition: A phenolic compound with strong antioxidant and tyrosinase-inhibiting properties that makes it an effective brightening and anti-hyperpigmentation agent. It has also been shown to be more potent than kojic acid in reducing melanin synthesis, and additionally possesses antimicrobial properties that can help control acne-causing bacteria on the skin's surface.

At this concentration, octocrylene functions as a UVB and short UVA filter that absorbs high-energy UV radiation and dissipates it as heat, protecting the skin from sunburn and photoaging. It is highly photostable and frequently used to stabilize other UV filters — particularly avobenzone — that degrade rapidly upon UV exposure, enhancing the overall durability and broad-spectrum coverage of a sunscreen formulation.

benefits: Hydrating, Reduces Irritation, Reduces Redness, Good for Scar Healing, Helps with Barrier Repair
ingredient_definition: A provitamin that converts to pantothenic acid in the skin, where it plays a key role in the synthesis and regeneration of lipids that form the skin barrier. It is a powerful humectant that attracts and retains moisture, has well-documented wound-healing and anti-inflammatory properties, and helps soothe irritated or sensitized skin.

benefits: Reduces Redness, Reduces Irritation
ingredient_definition: A flavonoid antioxidant found abundantly in plants that scavenges free radicals and inhibits inflammatory pathways, helping to protect the skin from oxidative stress and environmental damage. It also has anti-glycation properties, helping to prevent the cross-linking of collagen fibers caused by sugar exposure, and can help calm reactive or histamine-sensitive skin.

benefits: Helps with Anti-Aging, Reduces Irritation
ingredient_definition: A triterpenoid from Centella asiatica that inhibits collagen-degrading enzymes while simultaneously stimulating new collagen production, supporting both anti-aging and skin repair goals. It exerts significant anti-inflammatory effects, reduces redness, and is particularly effective in formulations targeting wound healing, barrier restoration, and skin sensitized by conditions like rosacea or eczema.

benefits: Helps with Anti-Aging, Hydrating, Good for Scar Healing, Good for Texture
ingredient_definition: A naturally occurring tripeptide-copper complex with potent skin regeneration, wound-healing, and anti-inflammatory properties. It promotes collagen, elastin, and glycosaminoglycan production, activates skin remodeling processes, and has antioxidant activity, making it one of the most biologically active peptide complexes used in advanced anti-aging skincare.

benefits: Brightening, Reduces Large Pores, Good for Texture, Reduces Redness
ingredient_definition: A highly lipophilic, oil-soluble vitamin C derivative with exceptional stability that penetrates the skin's lipid-rich layers far more efficiently than water-soluble vitamin C forms. It converts to active ascorbic acid in the skin to provide antioxidant protection, stimulate collagen synthesis, and inhibit melanin production, making it among the most potent and bioavailable vitamin C derivatives available.

At this concentration, octisalate functions as a UVB-absorbing chemical sunscreen that converts UV energy into heat, preventing it from damaging skin cells and DNA. It is commonly combined with other filters to create a well-rounded broad-spectrum formula, and its emollient texture also improves the cosmetic feel and spreadability of sunscreen formulations.

benefits: Helps with Barrier Repair
ingredient_definition: The acetate ester form of tocopherol that provides significantly greater formulation stability than free tocopherol, reducing the risk of oxidation during storage. Upon skin absorption, it is hydrolyzed to release free tocopherol, delivering antioxidant protection against UV and free radical-induced lipid peroxidation, along with skin-softening and barrier-supporting benefits.

benefits: Helps with Anti-Aging, Brightening, Reduces Large Pores, Reduces Redness, Good for Scar Healing, Helps with Dark Spots, Good for Texture
ingredient_definition: Pure L-ascorbic acid is the most biologically active form of vitamin C, directly and potently inhibiting tyrosinase, neutralizing free radicals, and stimulating collagen synthesis. It is the gold standard for topical vitamin C but requires careful formulation at low pH and in the absence of air and light to maintain stability and efficacy.

benefits: Helps with Anti-Aging, Hydrating, Reduces Irritation, Helps with Barrier Repair
ingredient_definition: Ceramide EOS is a unique ceramide subtype that serves as a linker molecule in the cornified envelope, anchoring the lipid lamellae to the protein scaffold of the stratum corneum. Replenishing ceramide EOS in topical formulations helps restore the structural integrity of this anchoring system, improving barrier function, reducing transepidermal water loss, and increasing skin resilience.

benefits: Helps with Anti-Aging
ingredient_definition: Commercially known as Matrixyl, this is one of the most widely studied anti-aging peptides, functioning as a matrikine to signal fibroblasts to produce collagen, elastin, and hyaluronic acid. Long-term use has been shown in multiple studies to visibly reduce wrinkle depth and volume, making it a cornerstone ingredient in evidence-based anti-aging formulations.

An oil-soluble organic UV filter that absorbs UVB and short UVA radiation while acting as a photostabilizer for other less stable UV filters, particularly avobenzone. It contributes broad-spectrum coverage, enhances water resistance in sunscreen formulations, and improves the overall elegance and wearability of sunscreen products.

benefits: Brightening, Good for Texture, Hydrating, Helps with Anti-Aging, Helps with Dark Spots
ingredient_definition: The biologically active, naturally occurring form of lactic acid and the most gentle of the commonly used AHAs, working to dissolve the bonds between dead skin cells to exfoliate the surface and improve texture. It is also a component of the skin's natural moisturizing factor, giving it dual functionality as both an exfoliant and a humectant, making it especially suitable for dry or sensitive skin.

benefits: Helps with Anti-Aging, Helps with Barrier Repair, Reduces Redness
ingredient_definition: A conjugate of salicylic acid and phytosphingosine that combines the keratolytic and anti-acne properties of salicylic acid with the ceramide-precursor and anti-inflammatory benefits of phytosphingosine. It helps reduce acne-causing bacteria, normalize sebum production, calm inflammation, and reinforce the lipid barrier simultaneously, making it a multifunctional ingredient for oily and blemish-prone skin.

benefits: Hydrating, Helps with Barrier Repair
ingredient_definition: A ceramide precursor formed by the conjugation of caproic acid and phytosphingosine that the skin can convert into ceramide-like lipids essential for barrier repair. It has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties and helps restore the lipid composition of a compromised stratum corneum, reducing sensitivity and improving overall skin barrier function.

An organic UVB filter that absorbs UV radiation in the 295-315 nm range and converts it to heat, protecting the skin from sunburn and UV-induced photodamage. It is commonly used in sunscreen formulations as a solubilizer for other UV filters like avobenzone and as a carrier to improve product texture and application feel.

benefits: Reduces Irritation, Reduces Redness, Hydrating, Good for Oily Skin
ingredient_definition: The most abundant and bioactive catechin found in green tea, EGCG is a potent antioxidant that neutralizes reactive oxygen species and reduces UV-induced oxidative damage. It also has anti-inflammatory and anti-carcinogenic properties, and can help reduce sebum production and inhibit acne-causing bacteria, giving it broad utility across anti-aging, sensitive skin, and acne-targeted formulations.

benefits: Brightening, Reduces Large Pores, Good for Texture
ingredient_definition: A stable, membrane-permeable vitamin C derivative in which an ethyl group at the 2-position protects the molecule from oxidative degradation while preserving its ability to convert to active ascorbic acid in the skin. It effectively inhibits melanin synthesis, supports collagen production, and provides antioxidant protection with better stability than pure vitamin C, suitable for both brightening and anti-aging applications.

benefits: Reduces Irritation, Reduces Redness
ingredient_definition: A synthetic peptide that modulates the skin's neuroimmune response by inhibiting the release of neuropeptides such as substance P, which trigger inflammatory and sensitization cascades in the skin. This neurocalming mechanism makes it particularly effective in soothing reactive, stressed, or hypersensitive skin, and it is often incorporated into formulations addressing redness, discomfort, and barrier vulnerability.

At this regulated concentration, avobenzone is one of the only chemical UV filters approved for broad UVA protection in many global markets including the United States. Because it is prone to photodegradation upon UV exposure, it is typically paired with photostabilizers such as octocrylene or homosalate to maintain its protective capacity throughout wear.

benefits: Helps with Anti-Aging
ingredient_definition: A signal peptide composed of glycine, histidine, and lysine that plays a role in tissue repair signaling and stimulates the production of collagen, elastin, and glycosaminoglycans. It helps improve skin firmness and elasticity, supports the skin's natural regeneration processes, and when complexed with copper, exhibits significantly enhanced biological activity and wound-healing properties.
